data.title: Prosus to Acquire Just Eat Takeaway for $4.29 Billion, Creating EU Food Delivery Giant
data.description:
Prosus N.V., the global technology company, has announced its intention to acquire Just Eat Takeaway.com N.V., the leading European online food delivery service, in a deal valued at approximately $4.29 billion. The acquisition, which is subject to customary pre-offer and offer conditions, including obtaining regulatory approvals, will create the world's fourth-largest food delivery group, with a strong presence in key European markets.

Just Eat Takeaway.com operates in 17 international markets, connecting 61 million customers with over 356,000 local partners. In 2024, the company generated €26.3 billion in Gross Transaction Value (GTV) and delivered an adjusted EBITDA of €460 million. Prosus, with its extensive food delivery portfolio outside of Europe, sees an opportunity to extend the leadership of a strong European food delivery platform and complement its existing footprint.

Prosus' acquisition of Just Eat Takeaway.com aligns with its investment philosophy, particularly in terms of stability, predictability, and consistent growth. The acquisition provides Prosus with a diversified market presence, a strong and established customer base, and consistent financial performance. Additionally, Prosus's proven track record in successfully scaling ecommerce platforms, as seen with iFood in Brazil, positions it well to invest in and accelerate growth at Just Eat Takeaway.com, unlocking value beyond its standalone potential as a listed business.

The acquisition also brings several strategic advantages to Just Eat Takeaway.com. Prosus's AI capabilities, which have been fundamental to the success of iFood, can improve the customer and driver experience, boost service reliability, and optimize logistics at Just Eat Takeaway.com. Prosus's extensive resources can further accelerate Just Eat Takeaway.com's investments and growth across food, groceries, fintech, and other adjacencies. Additionally, Prosus's global footprint complements Just Eat Takeaway.com's leading brand position in key European markets, creating a European tech champion and the world's fourth-largest food delivery group.

The proposed acquisition will significantly impact the competitive landscape in the European food delivery market. The combined entity will become a formidable competitor, potentially challenging the dominance of other major players like Uber Eats and Deliveroo. This could lead to further consolidation in the market, as other major players may feel the need to respond to the new competitive dynamics. This could lead to mergers and acquisitions among the remaining players, further reshaping the market landscape.

In conclusion, Prosus's acquisition of Just Eat Takeaway.com is a strategic move that aligns with Prosus's investment philosophy and brings several advantages to both companies. The acquisition will create a strong competitor in the European food delivery market, potentially reshaping the competitive landscape and putting pressure on other major players to innovate and adapt. As an investor, keeping an eye on this acquisition and the subsequent market developments can provide valuable insights into the future of the European food delivery market and the potential for growth and consolidation.

data.title: Innodata Soars 3.44%: FY 2025 Revenue Growth Forecasted at 40%+
CWCO: Seeking Alpha's 1.99% Surprise!
This 5.6%-Yielding Dividend Stock Keeps Finding New Ways to Grow
data.description:
In the ever-evolving world of investing, it's essential to stay adaptable and open to new opportunities. One such opportunity lies in the realm of dividend growth stocks, which offer a unique blend of income and capital appreciation. Today, we're going to explore a 5.6%-yielding dividend stock that has consistently found new ways to grow and outperform the broader market.



Brookmont Capital Management, a registered investment advisor, has been at the forefront of this trend with its Dividend Growth Strategy. This strategy focuses on investing in companies that consistently increase their dividends over time, providing a steady stream of income and the potential for significant capital appreciation. The firm's approach has paid off handsomely, with the strategy outperforming the Russell 1000 Value Index by a significant margin over the past five years.

So, what sets Brookmont's Dividend Growth Strategy apart, and how has it managed to consistently outperform the broader market? The answer lies in the firm's commitment to quality and its ability to identify companies with strong fundamentals and a history of dividend growth.



First and foremost, Brookmont prioritizes investing in companies with solid financial health and a track record of consistent dividend growth. This focus on quality ensures that the dividends are sustainable and less likely to be cut or suspended during market downturns. By reinvesting the dividends received from its portfolio, Brookmont is able to accelerate the growth of its assets and, consequently, its dividend payouts.

Another key factor contributing to Brookmont's outperformance is the firm's ability to adapt to changing market conditions. As interest rates fall, dividend stocks become increasingly attractive, as their yields become more competitive compared to declining bond yields. This dynamic has contributed to the broader market returns and further amplified the outperformance of dividend growth stocks.

In addition to its focus on quality and adaptability, Brookmont's Dividend Growth Strategy benefits from the firm's commitment to diversification. By maintaining a diversified portfolio, Brookmont reduces the impact of any single security on the overall performance of the portfolio. This diversification also helps to ensure the sustainability of the dividend growth, as it reduces the risk of a significant decline in any one sector or asset class.



In conclusion, Brookmont Capital Management's Dividend Growth Strategy has consistently outperformed its peers and the broader market due to its focus on quality dividend payers, the favorable impact of falling interest rates, and the long-term potential of consistent dividend growth. By investing in companies with strong fundamentals and a history of dividend growth, Brookmont has been able to generate steady income and significant capital appreciation for its clients.

If you're looking for a way to generate income and grow your wealth over the long term, consider exploring dividend growth stocks and the strategies employed by Brookmont Capital Management. By staying adaptable, focusing on quality, and maintaining a diversified portfolio, you too can find new ways to grow your portfolio and achieve your financial goals.

data.title: Upstart Surges 1.6% After Lawsuit News
ASX February 2025's Top Undervalued Small Caps With Insider Activity
data.description:
In the dynamic world of the Australian Securities Exchange (ASX), identifying undervalued small-cap stocks with insider activity can be a goldmine for savvy investors. As we step into February 2025, let's explore three compelling opportunities that align with the investment philosophy of favoring stability, predictability, and consistent growth.



1. Rural Funds Group (ASX:RFF)
RFF, a rural land and infrastructure investment company, is trading at a significant discount with a PE ratio of 7.9x and a PS ratio of 5.9x. Its Discount to Fair Value stands at 32.92%, suggesting that the market may have overlooked its true potential. With earnings growth projected at 26.2%, RFF's focus on rural land and infrastructure positions it well to benefit from long-term structural tailwinds. Recent insider buying activity, with more shares bought than sold, signals confidence in the company's future prospects.

2. Infomedia (ASX:IFM)
IFM, a digital media and technology solutions provider, is another undervalued small cap with a high PE ratio of 42.4x and a low PS ratio of 3.8x. Its Discount to Fair Value is 30.01%, indicating that the market may have overlooked its growth potential. With earnings growth projected at 24.8%, IFM's focus on digital media and technology solutions positions it well to benefit from the growing demand for digital content and services. Recent insider buying activity, with more shares bought than sold, suggests that insiders are confident in the company's future prospects.



3. Collins Foods (ASX:CKF)
CKF, a quick-service restaurant operator, is trading at a discount with a PE ratio of 18.5x and a low PS ratio of 0.6x. Its Discount to Fair Value is 1.39%, suggesting that the company may be undervalued. With earnings growth projected at 12.5%, CKF's focus on the quick-service restaurant sector positions it well to benefit from the growing demand for convenience food and dining experiences. Recent insider buying activity, with more shares bought than sold, indicates that insiders are confident in the company's future prospects.



In conclusion, these undervalued small caps with insider activity present attractive investment opportunities for investors seeking stability, predictability, and consistent growth. By staying informed about their fundamentals, growth prospects, and insider activity, investors can make well-informed decisions and potentially reap significant rewards. As always, it is essential to conduct thorough research and consider your risk tolerance before making any investment decisions.

data.title: 5 Fundamentally Solid Stocks That Are Oversold and Poised for Impulsive Rally (With Bonus Sell List)
data.description:
The sharp decline in the U.S. stock market last week has pushed risk sentiment to levels last seen on January 13th. At that time, stocks completed a correction, and the S&P 500 rebounded 5% within a week. While it's uncertain if the current round of sell-offs has ended, many fundamentally solid stocks have reached extremely oversold levels, positioning them for a potential short-term dead cat bounce. These stocks could serve as a hedge against further downside risk and may significantly outperform during a market recovery—whether driven by technical or fundamental factors. We've also included a list of stocks showing warning signs as a bonus at the end of the article, making them potential candidates for short positions.

Amazon (AMZN)

Amazon has dropped to the December 4th price level, a critical resistance point. Just last month, shares hovered around $216 before bouncing back. The stock has now corrected nearly 10% since its latest earnings, driven by a combination of disappointing guidance and valuation concerns. Despite the negative sentiment, the RSI has fallen to 16.8, a level last seen on August 5th, when a significant sell-off also coincided with a sharp oversold bounce to new highs. Given these factors, Amazon is in our buy-the-dip list, poised for a potential rebound in the short term, even if broader market conditions remain weak.

The Trade Desk (TTD)

The Trade Desk is another stock facing extreme pessimism. After a 40% drop over two weeks, driven by Q4 revenue and guidance misses, investor sentiment is at a low point. However, as a key supplier to Netflix and an emerging player in AI-powered advertising, The Trade Desk's fundamentals remain solid. Its RSI has now fallen to 10.2, last seen in November 2022, when the stock rallied 30% in just one week. The trading volume has also been down for five consecutive days, indicating reduced selling pressure. Given the low RSI and solid fundamentals, we expect a sharp bounce soon.

Walmart (WMT)

Walmart faced a significant 2-day sell-off after issuing weaker-than-expected guidance, citing tariff risks and a conservative outlook. Despite this, we view it as another buy-the-dip opportunity. The stock had previously surged as investors viewed it as a safe haven asset with strong pricing power. After the nearly 9% drop from pre-earnings levels, more funds are likely to flow into Walmart as a hedge against uncertainty. The RSI has fallen to 20, not extremely oversold but the lowest level in 10 months. If it continues to decline, more investors may step in to buy, given the company's robust consumer spending and self-reliance.

Caterpillar (CAT)

Caterpillar's stock has been negatively impacted by concerns over the U.S. economic outlook. The stock has retreated to September levels, but global manufacturing is still growing, as evidenced by a rise in global manufacturing PMI to 51.6 in February. Caterpillar remains well-positioned to mitigate tariff risks due to its status as a net exporter. The stock has an RSI of 13, not its lowest level historically but still showing oversold conditions. As economic prospects improve, Caterpillar, as a key player in global manufacturing, should benefit from a recovery in demand.

Block (XYZ)

Block saw its shares plunge 18% on its worst trading day after Q4 earnings missed expectations. However, the company's core business remains strong, with solid payment volume and EBITDA results. Despite missing revenue and earnings targets, the company reiterated its 2025 guidance. The RSI has dropped to 7.2, an extremely oversold level reminiscent of September 25, 2023. While the rebound may not be as swift or as large as The Trade Desk's, the stock is worth considering for a buy on the dip due to its strong fundamentals, though the bounce may take longer to materialize.

Bonus: Sell List (with Brief Comments)

Alibaba: RSI at 94, rally momentum fading; good news already priced in, hedge funds likely to cash out.

PDD: Faces Trump's tariff risks; rally driven by optimism around China AI but lacks substantial AI features.

Broadcom: MA (3, 7, 10) turning downward; lower highs forming on the chart.

Tesla: Musk's heavy political involvement raises concerns; AI and robotaxi take time, earnings warning possible, limited business benefits under Trump due to conflict-of-interest risks.

Uber: MA (3) at an inflection point; lacks momentum to surpass October's highs and may face further struggles.

data.title: Carrier Soars: Global Outlook Brightens
Paymentus Plunges 1.57%: Q4 Earnings Preview

Chorus Ltd (CHRYY): Navigating Economic Challenges with Resilience
data.description:
As Chorus Ltd (CHRYY) wraps up its H1 2025 earnings call, investors are left with a clear picture of the company's resilience in the face of ongoing economic challenges. The New Zealand-based telecommunications services provider has demonstrated a strong commitment to operational efficiency and customer satisfaction, positioning itself as a reliable choice for investors seeking stable, predictable growth.



Chorus Ltd's strategic reset in 2024 has been instrumental in driving its resilience. The company has focused on managing costs tightly, reducing its total employee numbers, and delivering steady financial results. This cost management strategy has enabled Chorus to maintain profitability and financial stability, even as the broader economy faces headwinds.

In addition to its focus on cost management, Chorus has also prioritized improving customer satisfaction. The company has worked closely with retailers to enhance processes and communications with consumers, leading to an average fiber installation rating of 8.2 out of 10 in 2021. This improvement in customer satisfaction has likely helped Chorus retain and attract more customers, further strengthening its competitive position in the New Zealand telecommunications market.



As Chorus continues to expand its fiber network, it is well-positioned to capitalize on the growing demand for high-speed internet services. The company's UFB rollout is ahead of schedule and 95% complete, with just 53,000 homes and businesses remaining to pass by the end of 2022. This continued expansion of its fiber network will enable Chorus to connect more customers and offer faster, more reliable internet services, driving growth in its customer base.

Moreover, Chorus's focus on diversification and innovation has opened up new revenue streams and further strengthened its competitive position. The company has been exploring opportunities in co-location and value-added network services, allowing it to tap into new markets and expand its service portfolio.



In conclusion, Chorus Ltd (CHRYY) has demonstrated remarkable resilience in the face of ongoing economic challenges, thanks to its strategic focus on operational efficiency, customer satisfaction, and network expansion. The company's strong financial position, dividend growth history, and commitment to innovation make it an attractive option for investors seeking stable, predictable growth in the telecommunications sector. As Chorus continues to navigate the economic landscape, it is well-positioned to capitalize on opportunities and deliver value to its shareholders.

data.title: Toutiao's inaugural platform governance open day: actively exploring AI content governance
data.description: On February 24, the platform governance open day event was held in Beijing. Relevant business leaders shared the measures, achievements, and planning of Toutiao in three aspects: positive communication, platform security, and content ecology.Since 2023, the platform has adhered to the concept of "spreading true information" and taken "hard-core science popularization" as the main direction of communication. It has launched the "Toutiao Talks About the Truth" project, which has released more than 36 billion views on the theme of science popularization and debunking. It has become an important part of the health and high-quality content ecology of Toutiao.In terms of safe operation, in recent years, Toutiao has established a complete "risk discovery → risk identification → risk disposal" full-process governance system, adhering to the principle of "early identification, multiple interception, multiple prompts, and rapid intervention", and has increased the governance intensity of the same content publishing, network fraud, and water army.For false rumors, Toutiao has established three core governance capabilities early, including rumor library, authoritative source library, and professional review team. In 2024, it added authoritative media source labels under the published content to provide users with more objective perspectives while ensuring the authenticity of information. In response to the rapid development of AI technology and the emergence of low-quality content in recent years, the platform encourages users to use AI to improve efficiency while protecting original space and upgrading and iterating the identification capabilities of AI models in a timely manner to tighten the review of AI content.It is reported that this is the first time Toutiao has held a platform governance open day. Earlier, Toutiao released the "2024 Annual Governance Report", which publicly announced the governance results of the platform against key specialties such as network false rumors, same content publishing, network fraud, bottom-line chasing of traffic, and water army in the past year. The report shows that the platform has intercepted more than 5 million untrue information, more than 930,000 low-quality AI content, and punished more than 7.81 million same content publishing. It has intercepted and disposed of more than 2 million suspected fraudulent content.It is known that in the future, Toutiao will continue to focus on platform security and ecological governance to create a clear and healthy Internet environment for society and users.

data.title: Perseus Mining Ltd: Half Year 2025 Earnings Call Highlights - Strong Gold Production and Cost Management
data.description:
Perseus Mining Ltd (PMNXF) recently reported its Half Year 2025 earnings, highlighting strong gold production and cost management strategies that have contributed to its overall financial performance and shareholder value. The company's revenue increased by 19% to US$581.8 million, while profit after tax grew by 22% to US$201.1 million. This impressive growth can be attributed to several key factors.

Firstly, Perseus Mining's gold production for the half year totalled 253,709 ounces at an All-In Site Cost (AISC) of US$1,162 per ounce. This result included significant contributions from its Yaouré, Sissingué, and Edikan mines. The company's strong hedging strategy and improving gold price environment have allowed it to benefit from higher sales prices, further enhancing its financial performance.

Secondly, Perseus Mining's net cash from operating activities for the half year ended December 31, 2024, increased by 17% to US$247.6 million. This indicates strong cash flow management and operational efficiency, as the company continues to generate substantial cash from its operations.

The company's net cash and bullion position also improved, with a balance of US$704 million, up by US$117 million from June 30, 2024. This strong net cash position allows Perseus Mining to fund growth and shareholder returns, as evidenced by its declaration of an interim dividend of Australian dollar (AUD) 2.5 cents per share, a 100% increase on H1 FY24 interim return.

Perseus Mining's CEO, Jeff Quartermain, attributed the company's success to its ability to maintain production levels and achieve market guidance for both production and costs. The company's diversified operations, growing production, strong balance sheet, and successful exploration efforts are expected to continue supporting long-term growth and profitability.



In conclusion, Perseus Mining Ltd's Half Year 2025 earnings call highlights the company's strong gold production and cost management strategies, which have contributed to its overall financial performance and shareholder value. The company's ability to maintain production levels, achieve market guidance, and generate substantial cash from operations positions it well for continued growth and success in the long term.

data.title: NVIDIA Earnings Loom: A Make-or-Break Moment for AI and U.S. Markets
data.description:
As NVIDIA prepares to release its highly anticipated fourth-quarter earnings report for the fiscal year 2024, investors and tech enthusiasts are paying close attention to the global leader in graphics processors and pioneer in the artificial intelligence (AI) domain. Expectations for the quarter ending February 26 indicate revenue of $410 billion, slightly below the market consensus of $420 billion, with a significant slowdown in revenue growth to 73%.

NVIDIA's earnings report not only reflects its own performance but also serves as a barometer for the health of the entire AI industry. Analysts caution that should NVIDIA's performance or guidance fail to meet expectations, it could lead to widespread turbulence in the AI sector and even impact the stability of the U.S. stock market.

On the eve of NVIDIA's earnings announcement, there is already a sense of unease in the U.S. stock market. The VIX index, which measures market volatility, spiked on Friday, along with sharp declines across the major indices. Notably, hedge fund titan Steve Cohen issued a warning that U.S. equities could experience a pullback, with economic growth possibly slowing to 1.5%.

The focus of the earnings report will be on AI data center revenue growth, product transition within inventory, and perspectives on AI application deployment. Despite robust global demand for AI chips, NVIDIA's outlook is complicated by intensifying competition. Additionally, delays in the delivery of its Blackwell series chips have the potential to affect overall revenue and gross margin.

According to Morgan Stanley, should NVIDIA's revenue exceed expectations, its stock price may rise by 3% to 15%; conversely, if performance falls short, the stock could drop by 5% to 10%. It's important to note that since the advent of ChatGPT, NVIDIA has been a key driver of the S&P 500 index, and its earnings report will directly influence the market performance of related chip giants like TSMC and SK Hynix.

Overall, NVIDIA's earnings report is a critical indicator for the AI industry, with its future performance having significant implications for the technology market. This anticipation is coupled with high expectations and concerns among investors regarding NVIDIA's trajectory. For automakers and potential buyers alike, advancements in AI technology are keenly observed, as they will directly impact future innovations in electric vehicles, autonomous driving, and smart car features.

In summary, NVIDIA's imminent earnings release will not only reveal its market standings but will also prompt new reflections and expectations on technological advancements within the automotive industry. The market's response remains to be seen.

data.title: Why Buffett Holds a Record $334.2 Billion in Cash?
data.description:
Buffett is renowned for his stock-picking acumen, yet nowadays, he shows an increasing inclination towards holding cash. Two days ago, Berkshire Hathaway, the company under Buffett's leadership, released its fourth-quarter and full-year financial reports for 2024, accompanied by Buffett's annual letter to shareholders.

As of the end of 2024, the total amount of cash and cash equivalents held by this renowned investor's company, Berkshire Hathaway, reached a staggering $334.2 billion, setting a new historical high. The proportion of these assets in the company's portfolio is also the highest since 1998.

 

Maintaining a large cash reserve has always been a consistent practice of Berkshire. However, the continuous expansion of the cash reserve scale recently has drawn the attention of some market observers. By carefully studying Buffett's latest letter to shareholders, we can seek clues to understand the views of this 94-year-old on the stock market, as well as his perspectives on long-term strategies, market conditions, and diversified investment portfolios.

Where Does the Cash Come From?

One of the reasons for the cash accumulation is that Berkshire has sold a substantial amount of Apple stocks. In recent years, the valuation of Apple stocks has been much higher than when Buffett's company established its position from 2016 to 2018.

 

According to data from FactSet, starting from the end of 2023, Berkshire has reduced its shares in Apple for four consecutive quarters. In the fourth quarter of last year, Berkshire temporarily halted further sales of Apple shares.

This is because this portion of the position in Berkshire Hathaway's stock portfolio has already accounted for a significant proportion. When his designated successor, Greg Abel, eventually takes over the company, he will be responsible for this transition.

Some investors and analysts speculate that Buffett's conservative moves over the past year are not a judgment on the market but rather a preparation for future deployments by reducing overly large positions and accumulating cash. The massive amount of cash will provide the successor with room for trial and error, especially for this successor who has been hailed as a prophet by the world.

Historical experience shows that new management often engages in aggressive mergers and acquisitions due to performance pressure (such as Microsoft's acquisition of LinkedIn in the early days of Satya Nadella's tenure). However, Berkshire's $334.2 billion reserve is sufficient to offset the potential losses of any single transaction.

Why Hoard a Large Amount of Cash?

Buffett also seems to be well aware that his move of holding a large amount of cash has received significant attention from the market. In his letter to shareholders, he stated that although some commentators believe that Berkshire's cash position is extremely high, the vast majority of the funds are still invested in stocks, and this preference will not change.

Buffett emphasized, Berkshire's shareholders can rest assured that we will always invest the majority of our funds in stocks - mainly American enterprises, but many of these companies also have important businesses in the global market. Berkshire will never prefer to hold cash equivalents while ignoring the equity of high-quality enterprises, whether it is a controlling stake or a partial stake.

In fact, Berkshire's huge cash holdings have raised questions among shareholders and observers, especially in a situation where interest rates are expected to decline from multi-year highs. In recent years, the CEO and Chairman of Berkshire has expressed frustration about the expensive market and the scarcity of buying opportunities. Some investors and analysts have grown impatient with the lack of action and are seeking explanations.

1. The Valuation Trap

Long-time observers of Berkshire believe that the main reason why Buffett is hoarding cash is the currently high market valuation.

The current forward price-to-earnings ratio (P/E) of the S&P 500 is 22.3 times, which is a 20% premium compared to the 10-year average of 18.6 times. The valuation of technology stocks, in particular, deviates significantly from the historical median. In the fields where Buffett excels, it has become difficult to find bargains among high-quality large enterprises, making it challenging for Buffett to find investment targets that meet his investment criteria. Take Apple as an example; its P/E ratio exceeded 30 times at one point in 2024, prompting Buffett to continuously reduce his holdings to the current 28%. As Buffett emphasized in his letter, We rarely find ourselves in a position of opportunity, which is in line with his creed of being fearful when others are greedy. 

Among the industries Buffett is familiar with, the prices of the large, high-quality enterprises that the company is looking for have risen too high, and stock pickers cannot be confident that an investment will bring substantial returns to Berkshire and its shareholders.

Statistics from The Wall Street Journal show that Berkshire's return rate over the past decade (225%) has already lagged behind that of the S&P 500 (241%), confirming the dilemma of the curse of scale where it is difficult for large entities to perform well.

 

2. The Value Depression

In stark contrast to the US stock market, the five major Japanese trading houses (such as Itochu and Mitsubishi) that Berkshire has heavily invested in have an average P/E ratio of only 5-8 times, a dividend yield of 3%-6%, and have achieved arbitrage through zero-interest-rate bond financing in Japan. As of the end of 2024, this investment has a floating profit of $9.7 billion, and the annual dividend income reaches $812 million. This cross-market valuation gap reveals Buffett's logic of exchanging cash for time: shrinking the front line in the US stock market bubble and shifting to Asian assets with a greater margin of safety.

 

Moreover, it is likely that the investment in Japanese assets will continue to increase in the future, and his successor will also hold Japanese assets for decades to come.

3. The Cushion

This scale is reminiscent of the classic case during the 2008 financial crisis when Buffett injected $10 billion in preferred stock into Goldman Sachs, with an annual return rate of 17%. In the current environment of geopolitical turmoil and the intensifying risk of future AI regulation, the cash reserve is essentially a put option, reserving the initiative to capture asymmetric returns.

4. The Arbitrage Space at the End of the Interest Rate Cycle

Although the market's expectation of the Federal Reserve's interest rate cuts is on the rise, the yield of short - term US Treasury bonds still remains close to 4.5%. Based on the estimation of Berkshire's cash reserves in the first three quarters of 2024, the interest income alone exceeded $10 billion. In the context where the yield of low - risk assets is rarely higher than the dividend yield of the S&P 500 (1.5%), this cash cow model has become a practical choice for hedging against inflation.


The Dynamic Balance of Value Investing

Buffett reaffirmed in his letter, Berkshire will never prefer cash over the equity of high-quality enterprises. This statement echoes his argument during the Internet bubble in 1999 that cash is a call option. Although the current cash proportion has reached a historical peak, the growth in the value of non-listed controlled enterprises has offset the decline in stock holdings.

Compared with the preference for low-valuation assets in the 1970s (such as Berkshire Hathaway's textile factory), the current strategy places more emphasis on the stability of cash flow. The investment in Japanese trading houses reflects this shift: enterprises like Mitsubishi have a stable return on equity (ROE) of over 10% and have benefited from the dividend increase brought about by Japan's corporate governance reform. This combination of high dividend + low volatility is essentially an advanced substitute for cash.

Problems cannot be solved by hope; action must be taken - Buffett quoted Munger's maxim to explain the current strategy. This is similar to the history when the two of them remained inactive during the technology bubble in 2000 and eventually made a profit of $4 billion by bottom-fishing PetroChina in 2003.

Conclusion

Buffett's cash empire is essentially the ultimate response of a value investor to the market cycle. When the Buffett Indicator (the total market value of US stocks/GDP) breaks through the 200% warning line, this octogenarian has built an anti-fragile barrier with $334.2 billion in cash. This is both a long-term belief in the American Miracle and a calm hedge against short-term enthusiasm. As stated at the end of the letter, Berkshire's future will still be rooted in the United States, but our wallets will always be open to global value - in an era of valuation differentiation and order reconstruction, cash is not the end but the starting point of the next miracle.

data.title: Alibaba's $53 Billion AI Bet: Challenging Trump's Stargate Plan
data.description:
Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. has announced a massive investment of $53 billion in cloud computing and artificial intelligence (AI) infrastructure over the next three years, positioning itself as a major player in the global AI landscape and directly challenging President Trump's Stargate AI plan. The investment, which exceeds Alibaba's total AI and cloud spending over the past decade, underscores the company's commitment to AI-driven growth and its role as a leading global cloud provider.

The announcement comes as Alibaba faces regulatory challenges in China, with the company refocusing on AI, e-commerce, and cloud computing. During its latest earnings call, Alibaba CEO Eddie Wu Yongming described AI as an "once-in-a-generation" opportunity, stating that if artificial general intelligence (AGI) is achieved, the AI-relevant industry will likely become the world's largest industry. Alibaba's investment strategy aligns with this vision, aiming to capture the AI cloud opportunity and accelerate technological advancements.

Alibaba's investment will be deployed towards expanding the company's existing data centers and boosting its AI infrastructure to support the growing demand for AI applications. The investment will exceed Alibaba's total spending on AI and cloud computing over the past decade, and the Chinese enterprise aims to leverage the need for substantial computing power by modern AI models to establish itself as a provider of cloud-based AI solutions.

Alibaba Cloud, the company's cloud division, has experienced consistent growth and is now its fastest-growing business segment. In the quarter ending December 31, Alibaba Cloud reported an 11% year-over-year increase in revenue, with AI-related products achieving triple-digit growth for the sixth consecutive quarter. The company's strong financial performance, with a 7.6% year-over-year increase in revenue reaching 280.15 billion yuan for the period, has been well-received by investors, with Alibaba's Hong Kong-listed shares rising 0.7% to HK$139.40, marking their highest level since November 2021.

Alibaba's investment announcement comes as several major tech companies, including Microsoft and Meta, have also earmarked significant funds for AI initiatives. Microsoft plans to invest $80 billion in AI data centers this fiscal year, while Meta has allocated around $65 billion for similar initiatives over the year. In the domestic AI landscape, Chinese companies like ByteDance have also been investing heavily in AI, allocating over 150 billion yuan ($20.69 billion) in capital expenditure for this year, with a focus on AI.

Alibaba's investment in AI infrastructure is a strategic move that positions the company to capitalize on the growing demand for AI applications and cloud services. By investing in its cloud computing and AI capabilities, Alibaba can attract more customers, foster innovation, and maintain a competitive edge in the global AI market. The investment also highlights the company's commitment to long-term technological innovation and its role as a leading global cloud provider.




In conclusion, Alibaba's $53 billion investment in cloud computing and AI infrastructure is a significant strategic move that challenges President Trump's Stargate AI plan and positions the company as a major player in the global AI landscape. The investment underscores Alibaba's commitment to AI-driven growth and its role as a leading global cloud provider, while also highlighting the company's ability to adapt to regulatory challenges and capitalize on the growing demand for AI applications and cloud services.

data.title: NN Group's 2024 Earnings: A Missed Opportunity or a Blip on the Radar?
data.description:
As the financial world continues to evolve, so do the expectations placed upon companies like NN Group. The Dutch financial services giant recently reported its full-year 2024 earnings, and while the results were mixed, they certainly sparked some interesting conversations among investors and analysts alike. Let's dive into the numbers and explore what this means for NN Group's future.



NN Group's revenue for 2024 came in at €6.90 billion, a 33% decrease from the previous year. This decline can be attributed to a variety of factors, including market conditions, regulatory changes, and the company's strategic focus. However, it's essential to note that this decrease was accompanied by a 35% increase in net income, which highlights the company's ability to maintain profitability despite the revenue drop.

The company's earnings per share (EPS) missed analyst estimates by 1.9%, which raised some eyebrows in the investment community. This miss can be attributed to several factors, including the significant portion of non-operating costs (€1.15 billion) in the company's expenses, which accounted for a substantial part of its overall costs. Additionally, the slight increase in operating capital generation (OCG) compared to the previous year may not have been enough to offset the higher expenses and maintain the expected EPS growth.



Despite the EPS miss, NN Group's revenue streams have shown positive trends in recent years. The Netherlands Non-Life segment, for example, has consistently contributed a significant portion of the company's total revenue, with a total revenue of €3.84 billion (56% of total revenue) in 2023. Additionally, the Insurance Europe segment exceeded its OCG target of EUR 450 million one year ahead of schedule, mainly supported by strong pension performance and new business growth. This suggests that the company's European operations are performing well.



Another positive trend for NN Group is the continued improvement in customer satisfaction scores. In 2024, 8 out of 10 countries ranked at or above market average, indicating a strong focus on customer experience and retention. This positive trend in customer satisfaction scores is a testament to the company's commitment to providing a superior digital experience for its customers and agents.



NN Group has also made significant strides in its sustainability efforts. The company further increased its investments in climate solutions to EUR 12.8 billion in 2024, demonstrating a commitment to responsible investing and contributing to a sustainable future. Additionally, NN Group has supported 766 thousand people through community investment programs since 2022, further enhancing its social responsibility and reputation.



Despite the EPS miss, NN Group's solvency II ratio remained robust at 194% in 2024, mainly due to adverse market impacts and regulatory changes, which were partly offset by management actions. This strong solvency ratio indicates that the company is well-positioned to weather market fluctuations and regulatory changes.

In conclusion, NN Group's 2024 earnings report was a mixed bag, with a revenue decline and an EPS miss that raised some concerns among investors. However, the company's strong business performance, positive trends in revenue streams, customer satisfaction, and sustainability initiatives suggest that the EPS miss may be a blip on the radar rather than a missed opportunity. As NN Group continues to navigate the ever-changing financial landscape, investors should keep a close eye on the company's progress and remain optimistic about its long-term prospects.

data.title: Bitcoin's $90K Test Looms as Inflation Data Looms
data.description:
Bitcoin (BTC) enters the final week of February in a state of uncertainty, with traders and analysts debating whether the cryptocurrency can avoid a fresh dip below $90,000. Several key factors are at play, including US inflation data, gold's continued rally, and Bitcoin's own volatility and network activity.

Bitcoin's price has been stuck in a tight trading range, with liquidity piling up on either side of the spot price. Traders are mapping out potential support levels, with some eyeing a retest of the $90,000 mark. Popular trader CrypNuevo noted that liquidation levels are equal on both the upside and downside, suggesting a potential push by either bulls or bears. Fellow trader Roman, however, was less optimistic, predicting a return to the bottom of the multimonth trading range.

On weekly timeframes, trader Luca identified an incoming test of Bitcoin's bull market support band, formed by two moving averages. He suggested that Bitcoin was primed for a return to the upside amid low funding rates, poor sentiment, and retail investors reducing exposure. However, the overall market sentiment remains bearish, and network activity has been declining, raising concerns about potential trouble brewing.

This week, markets are awaiting the release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures (PCE) Index, the Federal Reserve's "preferred" inflation gauge. The data could offer further insight into the risk of stagflation, which has been a growing concern for investors. While the potential for stagflation is a major concern, historical data shows that it is not necessarily associated with poor stock market performance.

In contrast to Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies, gold continues to set new all-time highs and is working on its highest-ever daily close. The US dollar index (DXY) is seeking a reversal from a downtrend in place since early December. While a strong dollar typically pressures risk assets, the current landscape stands out on longer timeframes, with gold prices rising alongside the US Dollar and 10-year note yield.

Bitcoin's stubborn trading range has led to some rare readings from volatility metrics. On weekly timeframes, realized volatility is near record lows, approaching levels seen only a few times in the past four years. Similar compressions in the past have led to major market moves. data.title: Ethereum's Volatile Dance: Leveraged Traders Face Risks as Whales Diverge
data.description:
Ethereum (ETH) continues to experience a volatile market landscape, as highlighted by a recent analysis from Bitunix. Coinglass data reveals critical liquidation points for the cryptocurrency, specifically within the price range of $2,751 to $2,774. This situation poses significant risks for leveraged traders, who may face forced liquidations that could exacerbate existing price movements. Additionally, the whale holdings long/short ratio near parity suggests a notable divergence in market sentiment regarding future price directions, which could lead to pronounced oscillations.

The ETH perpetual contract funding rate remains marginally positive, indicating sustained bullish sentiment among investors. This reflects their willingness to maintain underlying positions. However, the stability of Ethereum spot ETF holdings implies that institutional investors are holding steady in their allocations. Looking ahead, the short-term outlook appears uncertain, with ETH potentially moving between $2,618 and $2,880, contingent on shifts in market dynamics. To navigate this volatile terrain, investors should exercise caution by implementing stop-loss strategies and critically monitoring market sentiment and funding rate fluctuations.

data.title: "Qubetics: Your Second Chance at Crypto Gains"
data.description:
Missed Out on Hedera’s Explosive Gains? Qubetics Offers Another Chance at Massive Returns

In the dynamic world of cryptocurrencies, investors often reminisce about the opportunities they missed, such as Bitcoin under $100 or Ethereum before smart contracts took off. Hedera, once a promising enterprise blockchain, is another example of a crypto that got away, delivering massive returns for early investors but leaving many to regret not getting in at the ground floor.

However, the crypto market is giving investors another shot at getting in early with Qubetics ($TICS), an innovative Web3 project revolutionizing blockchain development through its QubeQode IDE. With a booming presale, cutting-edge technology, and mind-blowing ROI potential, Qubetics is poised to become one of the top altcoins for massive return potential.

Qubetics is shaking up the industry with its revolutionary QubeQode IDE, a game-changer for blockchain developers and businesses. Think of it like the Google Docs of Web3 development, but instead of just editing text, developers can code, deploy, and manage smart contracts seamlessly across multiple blockchains. For businesses, this means faster and cheaper blockchain integration, while developers benefit from a one-stop solution to create multiple chains without the usual technical headaches.

The Qubetics presale has reached Stage 23, with the project generating excitement among crypto enthusiasts. Investors are locking in at the current price of $0.08880, knowing that a 10% price hike is scheduled for Sunday at 12 AM. With over $13.5 million raised, 20,800+ holders, and 483 million tokens sold, demand remains high as investors race to get in before the next price increase.

Market projections for Qubetics remain highly bullish, with analysts expecting $TICS to reach $0.25 by the end of the presale, offering an impressive 181.52% ROI. But the real upside comes post-presale, where analysts see a climb to $1 (1,026.09% ROI), $5 (5,530.44% ROI), and even $15 (16,791.32% ROI) following the mainnet launch in Q
